channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Five years since its inception, a US development agency competes with China on global projects
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When President Joe Biden visited Angola last week, one of the highlights was his pledge of hundreds of millions of dollars for an ambitious trans-Africa rail project that would bring copper and cobalt from central Africa to the Atlantic port of Lobito.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard, Trump's pick for intel chief, faces questions on Capitol Hill amid Syria fallout
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump’s pick for intelligence chief, Tulsi Gabbard, is making her case on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Farnoush Amiri, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Florida State Rep. Susan Valdes, representing parts of Hillsborough County, switches political parties
item.description: State Rep. Susan Valdes, who represents Florida's 64th District, including parts of Hillsborough County, switched political parties, the Florida House of Representatives announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump voices support for RFK Jr's vaccine views, suggests Kennedy will research debunked autism link
item.description: “When you look at some of the problems, when you look at what’s going on with disease and sickness in our country, something’s wrong,” Trump said on "Meet The Press.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk joins Trump allies in House GOP pressure campaign over social media bill
item.description: People in President-elect Trump's orbit are pushing Speaker Johnson and House Republicans to take up the Kids Online Safety Act.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court turns away Boston school admissions policy challenge as Alito, Thomas dissent
item.description: The Supreme Court turned away a group of white and Asian parents’ challenge to a temporary admissions policy at Boston’s prestigious “exam schools” over dissents from two of the court’s conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's tariff threats go beyond 'trade agreement' to advance American interests: expert
item.description: President-elect Trump’s proposed 25% tariff on Canada and Mexico imports aims to boost U.S. leverage, with one expert expecting targeted, strategic use.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump names several picks for State Department roles
item.description: In several posts on Truth Social, Trump shared the new positions to work under his nominated Secretary of State Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.).
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Trump names Alina Habba as counselor to president
item.description: “Alina has been a tireless advocate for Justice, a fierce Defender of the Rule of Law, and an invaluable Advisor to my Campaign and Transition Team,” Trump said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: The US believes journalist Austin Tice is alive after disappearing in Syria in 2012, Biden says
item.description: President Joe Biden says the U.S. government believes missing American journalist Austin Tice, who disappeared 12 years ago near the Syrian capital, is alive and Washington is committed to bringing him home after Bashar Assad’s ouster from power.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: House GOP to choose chairs for influential committees: Who’s in the running
item.description: House Republican leaders will choose new chairs for a number of influential committees this week, people who will have the power to help deliver on President-elect Trump’s priorities in the GOP trifecta over the next two years.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump back on the world stage at Notre Dame reopening in Paris
item.description: Zelenskyy said his first face-to-face encounter with Trump since the election was “good and productive,” adding they had agreed to continue working together.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Trump offers a public show of support for Hegseth and says he believes he can be confirmed
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump on Friday offered a public show of support for Pete Hegseth, his embattled choice to lead the Defense Department, whose confirmation by the Senate is in doubt as he faces questions over allegations of excessive drinking, sexual assault and his views on women in combat.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Johnson woos his sharpest critics in preparation for Speaker vote
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is wooing his sharpest critics in the House GOP ahead of his official election for Speaker on the House floor, working to eliminate opposition and secure the strongest leadership mandate possible in a razor-thin majority. In the latest development, Rep.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Former national security officials urge caution on Gabbard
item.description: A group of nearly 100 former national security officials urged the Senate to “carefully scrutinize” former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s nominee to serve as director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Mayor Adams on City of Yes plan passing City Council vote
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – The New York City Council has approved Mayor Eric Adams' ambitious City of Yes plan. The $5 billion plan will rezone the Big Apple for the first time in six decades. It's estimated to create more than 80,000 new housing units over 15 years.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Minnesota U.S. Attorney Andy Luger to step down ahead of Trump transition
item.description: U.S. Attorney Andy Luger is stepping down soon as the top federal law enforcement official in Minnesota. As is custom for political appointees, he’s resigning to make way for a yet-to-be-announced successor chosen by incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: Trump nominates Brandon Judd as ambassador to Chile
item.description: Brandon Judd was the president of the National Border Patrol Council, which represents around 18,000 Border Patrol agents and support personnel. He officially retired from the post on May 18.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Las Vegas Mayor Shelley Berkley sworn into office; envisions future of prosperity for the city
item.description: Shelley Berkley was sworn in as the mayor of Las Vegas on Wednesday, promising to serve the city well and work with the community to create an atmosphere of growth and prosperity.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Majority of Supreme Court seems reluctant to block state bans on medical treatments for minors
item.description: Hearing a high-profile culture-war clash, a majority of the Supreme Court seemed reluctant Wednesday to block Tennessee’s ban on gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court hears challenge to law banning gender-affirming care for trans kids
item.description: At issue is a Tennessee law that bans access to hormones, puberty blockers, and other treatments for trans kids in the state.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk's more than $50 billion pay deal at Tesla was rejected again. Here is why
item.description: A judge in Delaware has for the second time struck down a compensation package for Elon Musk after a Tesla shareholder filed suit.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: The fight against medical debt is pivoting to the states after Trump's election
item.description: Patient and consumer advocates fear a new Trump administration will scale back federal efforts to expand financial protections for patients and shield them from debt.
item.dc:creator: Noam Levey, MPR News

item.title: Unions score a major win in Wisconsin with a court ruling restoring collective bargaining rights
item.description: Wisconsin public worker and teachers unions have scored a major legal victory with a ruling that restores collective bargaining rights they lost under a 2011 state law.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden’s pardon of son Hunter threatens to tarnish legacy
item.description: President Biden’s decision to pardon his son Hunter in his final full month in the White House is threatening to erode his legacy as he prepares to leave office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Trump appointments set off special elections to fill House seats: What to know
item.description: The move by President-elect Trump to tap several House Republicans to fill positions in his Cabinet and beyond has set off a fresh round of special elections next year to replace them. Trump chose Reps. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.) and Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.) to be his national security adviser and U.S.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, Fox 5
